briefs.title: Development Cost Charge Instalment Rules Tightened for BC Developers Starting January 2026
briefs.summary: BC is changing how developers pay Development Cost Charges (fees charged by transit authorities when new buildings are approved). Starting January 1, 2026, developers who pay in instalments must pay 25% upfront on the approval or permit date, with the remaining balance due within 4 years — or sooner if occupancy permits (the final sign-off allowing a building to be used) are issued. Developers must also provide a financial guarantee, such as a surety bond or letter of credit, on that same date.
briefs.action_required: Review your development project payment plans and arrange a surety bond or letter of credit before January 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Development Cost Charge instalment rules take effect

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt
